summary: Iteration 6 comprehensive testing complete. Backend: 16/17 pytest cases pass (94%). Frontend: 30/30 acceptance checks pass. One real bug found: notifications._escape_md over-escapes by iterating '\\' last, so already-inserted escape backslashes get re-escaped, producing '\\.' instead of '\.' which Telegram MarkdownV2 will render literally. /api/events with whitelisted (trial_with_expert, exit_click) returns 201 with no exceptions; non-whitelisted names still persist. /api/webhooks/calendly works (received + duplicate_ignored idempotency + 400 invalid JSON). robots.txt and sitemap.xml served correctly with 5 <url> entries. Frontend: CountdownBanner visible, ticks d/h/m/s, --am-banner-h CSS var toggles 44px<->0px, countdown-cta + calculator-cta open TrialIntentModal, dismiss persists via localStorage am_combo_banner_dismiss_v1 and banner stays hidden on reload (verified without test init-script interference). ValueCalculator math verified: ticket=1000, closes=10, leads=50 -> projected=US$ 13.000, monthly=+US$ 3.000, annual=+US$ 36.000 (es-AR locale). All 4 SuccessCases cards render. Navbar shows Combo/Servicios/Proceso/Calculadora/Casos. Analytics no-op confirmed: window.gtag and window.fbq both undefined (env vars empty). No console errors. No horizontal overflow at 390px mobile viewport with banner active.

minor.issue: Double-escapes backslashes. The loop iterates reserved chars including '\\' last, so backslashes inserted while escaping earlier chars (e.g. '.', '-', '_') are themselves re-escaped, producing '\\.' instead of '\.'. Telegram MarkdownV2 renders the literal '\\.' instead of '.'. Fix: in notifications._escape_md, escape '\\' BEFORE the other chars, OR simply remove '\\' from the iteration list (raw booking/event payload values almost never contain a backslash).

action_items: Fix notifications._escape_md to avoid double-escaping the backslash. Recommended patch: replace the loop with `for ch in r'_*[]()~`>#+-=|{}.!': text = text.replace(ch, f'\\{ch}')` (drop the trailing '\\\\' from the char set). Verifies via the failing test: TestNotificationsModule.test_format_booking_message_contains_name_email_start.
critical_code_review_comments: notifications._escape_md has the double-escape bug described above — Telegram payloads will render with visible backslashes once TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN/CHAT_ID are populated in production. Fix before enabling Telegram notifications.

BackgroundTasks usage is correct: notification scheduling is wrapped in try/except, so background task scheduling failures cannot affect API response codes. Verified that with TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN/CHAT_ID missing, send_telegram_notification returns False without raising.
/api/events whitelisting (`if ev.name in ('trial_with_expert', 'exit_click')`) is code-reviewed and correctly excludes other event names from notifications while still persisting them.
Analytics.jsx correctly gates all script injection behind GA4_ID / PIXEL_ID env vars; renders null when unset — no global side-effects.
CountdownBanner sets --am-banner-h via useEffect AND inside the dismiss handler — both paths converge correctly; Navbar consumes the CSS var so top offset is reactive.

ValueCalculator math (`projectedCloses = min(leads, closes*1.3)`) caps lift at the available lead pool, which is conservative and matches the on-page disclaimer. Verified live with 1000/10/50 inputs producing 13.000 projected revenue.
Minor robustness: the empty `except` blocks around `localStorage.setItem` in CountdownBanner.jsx silently swallow QuotaExceeded — acceptable for a banner; fine as-is.
seed_data_creation: Test events created with name='trial_with_expert', 'exit_click', 'TEST_other_event_iter6'; test lead test_iter6@example.com; test Calendly bookings with TEST-iter6-* uuids. All persisted to MongoDB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite is at /app/backend/tests/test_iter6_notifications.py. Run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iter6_notifications.py -v. Notifications double-escape bug confirmed; once fixed re-run TestNotificationsModule. TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID intentionally unset in env — send_telegram_notification returns False. For frontend testing, do NOT use page.add_init_script to remove am_combo_banner_dismiss_v1 around reload tests — the init script runs on every navigation including reload, breaking persistence verification. Instead, manage localStorage directly via page.evaluate AFTER navigation. Calculator sliders are <input type='range'> — must dispatch 'input' event via native HTMLInputElement value setter (page.fill does not trigger React onChange).
rca of the issue: RCA — notifications._escape_md double-escape: The function iterates `for ch in r'_*[]()~`>#+-=|{}.!\\'` and replaces each occurrence with `f'\\{ch}'`. Reproduction: `_escape_md('a.b')` -> first iteration on '.' produces 'a\\.b'; the loop continues and when ch='\\', it replaces the just-inserted backslash with '\\\\', yielding 'a\\\\.b' (literal text: a, two backslashes, dot, b). Telegram MarkdownV2 will render this as `a\\.b` (visible backslash + dot) instead of the intended `a.b`. Mitigation: either move the backslash to be escaped FIRST (so subsequent insertions add backslashes that are not re-iterated), or drop '\\' entirely from the iteration since raw payload values (names, emails, ISO timestamps, timezone strings) practically never contain a backslash.
